definitions.example: For example, if we have data points 10, 20, 30, 40, and 50, the range is 40 (50 - 10).
definitions.definition: The difference between the highest and lowest values in a dataset, providing a rough estimate of dispersion. It does not consider the actual values of the data points.

definitions.example: Mean Deviation = (Σ|xi - mean|) / N, where xi is each data point, mean is the mean of the dataset, and N is the number of data points.
definitions.definition: The average of the absolute differences between each data point and the mean, providing a measure of dispersion by considering the deviations from the mean.

definitions.example: Variance = (Σ(xi - mean)^2) / N, where xi is each data point, mean is the mean of the dataset, and N is the number of data points.
definitions.definition: The average of the squared differences between each data point and the mean, providing a measure of dispersion by considering the squared deviations from the mean.
The square root of the variance, providing a measure of dispersion in the same unit as the data points, making it easier to interpret.

definitions.example: Coefficient of Range = Range / ((Highest value + Lowest value) / 2)
definitions.definition: The ratio of the range to the average of the highest and lowest values, providing a relative measure of dispersion.

definitions.example: Coefficient of Mean Deviation = Mean Deviation / Mean
definitions.definition: The ratio of the mean deviation to the mean, providing a relative measure of dispersion.
The ratio of the standard deviation to the mean, expressed as a percentage, providing a relative measure of dispersion.
The difference between the 75th percentile (Q3) and the 25th percentile (Q1), providing a measure of dispersion in the middle 50% of the data.
Half of the interquartile range, providing a measure of dispersion in the middle 50% of the data.

definitions.example: Coefficient of Quartile Deviation = Quartile Deviation / Median
definitions.definition: The ratio of the quartile deviation to the median, providing a relative measure of dispersion.

definitions.example: |xi - mean| or |xi - median|, where xi is each data point, mean or median is the central tendency.
definitions.definition: The absolute difference between each data point and the mean or median, providing a measure of dispersion by considering the deviations from the central tendency.

definitions.example: Root Mean Square Deviation = √((Σ(xi - mean)^2) / N), where xi is each data point, mean is the mean of the dataset, and N is the number of data points.
definitions.definition: The square root of the average of the squared differences between each data point and the mean, providing a measure of dispersion by considering the squared deviations from the mean.

definitions.example: Mean Absolute Deviation = (Σ|xi - mean|) / N, where xi is each data point, mean is the mean of the dataset, and N is the number of data points.
definitions.definition: The average of the absolute differences between each data point and the mean, providing a measure of dispersion by considering the deviations from the mean.

definitions.example: Standard Error = Standard Deviation / √N, where N is the sample size.
definitions.definition: The standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the sample mean, providing a measure of the variability of the sample mean.

definitions.example: Index of Dispersion = Variance of sample / Variance of population
definitions.definition: A measure of dispersion that compares the variance of a sample to the variance of a population, providing a measure of the variability of the sample.

definitions.example: Coefficient of Dispersion = Standard Deviation of sample / Standard Deviation of population
definitions.definition: A relative measure of dispersion that compares the standard deviation or variance of a sample to the standard deviation or variance of a population.
